title: Outdated dependencies detected in package
author: amritmmmec
body:
### Description 

  

We are using your package as a dependency in our project. 

  

In our internal security scan (conducted via **Black Duck**), we found that **some of the dependencies included in your package have not been updated for more than 12 months**. 

  

This includes both runtime and transitive dependencies that are part of your package's dependency tree. 

  

We are raising this issue to make you aware of this observation.  Are these outdated dependencies something you plan to update? 

  

### Outdated Dependency List (from Black Duck scan) 

  

The following are the dependencies (direct or transitive) in your package that were identified as outdated (last updated >12 months ago): 

  

 | Library Name   | Current Version | Package Name | Package Version | 

  |:--------------|:---------------:| :-----------:|----------------:|

comments.author: gameroman
comments.body:
Try one of these which are more actively maintained forks

https://github.com/excel-js/exceljs

https://github.com/cjnoname/excelts

https://github.com/protobi/exceljs

comments.author: Ben-CA
comments.body:
Would be nice if we could avoid these notices without switching to a fork.

**npm install exceljs**  
npm warn deprecated lodash.isequal@4.5.0: This package is deprecated. Use require('node:util').isDeepStrictEqual instead.
npm warn deprecated rimraf@2.7.1: Rimraf versions prior to v4 are no longer supported
npm warn deprecated fstream@1.0.12: This package is no longer supported.

comments.author: gameroman
comments.body:
> Would be nice if we could avoid these notices without switching to a fork.
> 
> **npm install exceljs** npm warn deprecated lodash.isequal@4.5.0: This package is deprecated. Use require('node:util').isDeepStrictEqual instead. npm warn deprecated rimraf@2.7.1: Rimraf versions prior to v4 are no longer supported npm warn deprecated fstream@1.0.12: This package is no longer supported.

You could probably use overrides, but I would recommend to try one of those forks since `exceljs` is unlikely to receive new updates in the near future
